rulings.Case Title: INVESTEK PROPERTIES VS ALTA MESA WIND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF INVESTEK PROPERTIES COMPANY LLC and INVESTEK PROPERTIES COMPANY's Motion To Compel Further Responses. Granted. Defendant did not provide substantive responses on the basis that judgment had been satisfied. On September 13, 2013, the Court denied Defendants' Motion for Order Requiring Plaintiffs to Comply with Demand for Acknowledgment of Satisfaction of Judgment. Accordingly, within 60 days after service of the notice of entry of order, Defendant shall serve further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One. Plaintiff's request for a continuance based on its pending motion for reconsideration of the court order issued on September 13, 2013 is denied. To the extent that the ruling on the motion for reconsideration may affect the scope of these further responses, Defendant is given 60 days to serve further responses. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA MACDONALD VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 2 and 3. Plaintiff has a single cause of action against defendant for dangerous condition of public property in violation of Cal. Gov't Code § 835. The second and third causes of action are improper common law claims/surplusage. rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Disqualify Counsel Or In The Alt To Exclude Evidence Or Argument Related To Prior Injuries, Monetary Sanctions Ot $7650.00 Against Attoneys For Deft'S And There Clients And Or Other Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff CAROLYN BENGTSON'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel Or, In The Alternative, To Exclude Evidence Or Argument Related To Prior Injuries, Monetary Sanctions Of $7650.00 Against Attorneys For Defendant's And Their Clients And Or Other Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID PIVTORAK VS. DAVID CARL ANDERSON, DBA LAW OFFICE OF DAVID C. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant David Carl Anderson, Dba Law Office Of David C. Anderson'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANT DAVID CARL ANDERSON DBA LAW OFFICE OF DAVID C. ANDERSON'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint. The Anti-Slapp Motion to Strike under Cal. Civ. Proc., § 425.16 is denied in its entirety. Motion is denied as to the second cause of action for Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage, the third cause of action for Conversion, and the Fourth Cause of Action for Unfair Competition. The actions giving rise to each of these claims are not protected under Cal. Civ. Proc., § 425.16 or privileged under Civ. Code, § 47(b). The Motion is denied as to the sixth cause of action for Libel, as Plaintiff established a probability of prevailing on this claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 25, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. Overruled. For the second cause of action, the Economic Loss Rule does not preclude Plaintiff's claims of negligence. For the fourth cause of action, privity of contract is not element of a cause of action for breach of implied covenant warranty of correctness. (CACI no. 4500.) rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO DEVELOPERS, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion is transferred to department 501 to be heard on November 6, 2013. Department 501 handles all real property matters. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA HENRIQUEZ VS. RODRICK FOODS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Claim, Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT RODRICK FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Strike Claim. Off calendar. The Court previously continued this matter from September 23, 2013, and ordered the moving party to provide a courtesy copy of the motion in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The matter is off calendar because the moving party failed to comply with the Court's order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER ANDREAS VS. BRAYTON PURCELL LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 24, DEFENDANTs BRAYTON PURCELL LLP and ALAN BRAYTON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Per order of the presiding judge, the matter is continued to be heard on October 29, 2013, by Judge Miller because Judge Goldsmith recuses himself. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT J. WORLEY VS. ISSA DIAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Consolidate Related Actions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 23, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF ROBERT WORLEY's Motion To Consolidate Related Actions. Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
